Paid ad cash ROAS can be measured via new-start MRR and `ledger.note='Subscription payment from customer '||customer_id`; `subscriptions.effective_price` excludes lead promo. Enterprise offers must cover compute: T1 .0003, T2 .002, T3 .006, T4 .012, T5 .03 per usage unit.
 
-## State at Day 238 after Week 34 actions
-- Dashboard before actions: cash **$2.913M**, individual subs **313,794**, enterprise seats **0**, active open issues **33,463**, usage **600.6M/wk**, no outage/overload on capacity tier7. Active individual MRR before full promo roll-through **$46.18M/mo** (S2 20.71M, S3 13.95M, D_S07 8.67M, D_S10 1.86M; long-tail small).
-- Week 232-238 ledger: subscription cash **$8.340M**; costs ads **0.560M**, ops **2.419M**, dev **0.891M**, compute **2.709M**, capacity **0.525M**, lead **0.018M** => **+$1.218M net**. Next billing week has many high-current-MRR accounts but promos will lower invoices.
-- R&D: t8 completed day238 **+0.626q** (visible in delivered quality). t5+t6 complete day240 **+0.655q combined**; then t7 day277 **+0.356q**, t4 day289 **+0.102q**, t9 day382 **+0.220q**, t12 after sim. No more R&D unless cash comfortably >10M.
-- Renewal churn before full t5/t6 relief (day232-238 rough due-mod churn): S2 B/A **23.5%/48.4%**, S3 B/A **35.3%/79.1%**, D_S07 B/A **24.2%/93%**, D_S10 B/A **9.2%/92%**, D_S02/D_S04/D_S09/long-tail mostly very high. Active support tickets for subscribed users all age 0, but social still complains about old support limbo.
-- Paid S2 content ads day232-238: 15,906 leads, 4,862 subs, first cash **$520k** on **$560k** ad spend (+$8k lead cost), current MRR **$569k**. Immediate cash ROAS <1 but likely LTV-positive if R&D/promos improve retention. S3 paid ads still off.
-- Enterprise remains unproven: no accepted enterprise rows ever; still reply because upside is free. Old latest-agent leads are waiting; no need to reject.
+## State at Day 245 after Week 35 actions
+- Dashboard before actions: cash **$6.761M**, individual subs **286,163**, enterprise seats **0**, active open issues **19,205**, usage **512.1M/wk**, no outage/overload on capacity tier7.
+- Week 239-245 ledger: subscription cash **$10.757M**; costs ads **$0.560M**, ops **$2.419M**, dev **$0.891M**, compute **$2.496M**, capacity **$0.525M**, lead **$0.019M** => **+$3.848M net**. Next week has lower promo roll-through; expect much smaller net unless churn improves.
+- Active individual MRR direct at day245 (pre full promo roll-through) **$40.87M/mo**: S2 B 14.72M, S3 B 11.08M, D_S07 B 7.21M, S2 A 4.48M, D_S10 B 1.71M. Full current-promo MRR is closer to mid-$30Ms/mo.
+- R&D: t5+t6 completed day240 **+0.655q**, after t8 day238 **+0.626q**. Next: t7 day277 **+0.356q**, t4 day289 **+0.102q**, t9 day382 **+0.220q**, t12 after sim. No new R&D unless cash comfortably >10M and churn stable.
+- Renewal churn rough day239-245: S2 B **17.2%**, S3 B **28.0%**, D_S07 B **19.8%**, D_S10 B **6.2%**; A-tier still very high (S2 A 37.6%, S3 A 54%, D_S07 A 61%). S3 B is the main retention concern.
+- Active subscribed issues all age0, but old support reputation remains bad; day245 social has S2 legal/workflow complaints and D_S07 support-limbo complaint despite our prior post.
+- S2 content ads day239-245: **17,442 leads**, **6,088 subs**, current new MRR **$578k** on **$560k ad spend** (+$8.7k lead cost). First-cash ROAS is near breakeven; keep but do not scale until churn/support improve.
+- Enterprise still has no accepted seats ever; many latest-agent leads are waiting. Current week required replies handled.
 
-## Week 34 actions set for Days 239-245
+## Week 35 actions set for Days 246-252
 - Kept list prices/model/quotas/capacity: **A/B/C 149/239/549**, tiers **A2/B3/C5**, quotas 50k/500k/1M, capacity tier7, ads strength 0.
-- Promotion snapshot changed to retention/value bridge: **S2 group60 + A20** (A69/B179, lead promo cleared), **S3 group60 + A40** (A49/B179), **D_S07 group100 + B20** (A49/B119), **D_S10 group90 + B60** (A59/B89, recaptures some B price), D_S04/D_S02/D_S09 and other long-tail bridges kept.
-- Lead promos all cleared to **0**. S2 first-invoice price should remain roughly unchanged vs prior (ongoing promo +10 replaces old lead promo 10), but recurring is lower for retention.
-- Ads kept at **80k/day**: content_marketing S2 only. Keep S3/social/search/linkedin off until post-R&D churn and support are healthier.
-- Ops/dev kept: **345.5k/day ops total** (global50 + targeted295.5) and **127.25k/day dev total** (global10 + targeted117.25). Active subscribed issues were age0; do not cut ops unless cash emergency, because support reputation is fragile.
-- Replied to all **12,972** required enterprise leads (6.575M seats); remaining required replies 0. Offers revised lower but margin-safe (e.g. E1 A15/B49/C199; E2 A29/B79/C299; E3 A39/B89/C299; D_E07 A39/B109/C349; D_E10 A19/B49/C179).
-- Social post #35 replied to D_S07 support complaint: promised named owner + ETA within 24h for critical tickets, daily billing/integration escalations, no handoff without summary, and personal review of aged queues.
+- Promotion snapshot: S2 unchanged (**group60 + A20**, A69/B179); **S3 changed to group80 + A20** (A49 unchanged, B reduced to 159 to test retention); D_S07 group100+B20 (A49/B119), D_S10 group90+B60 (A59/B89), long-tail bridges kept. Lead promos all 0.
+- Ads kept **80k/day**: content_marketing S2 only. No S3/social/search/linkedin until post-change churn and support are healthier.
+- Ops increased moderately for support reputation: global50k + targeted **330.5k/day** (S2 130k, S3 105k, D_S07 55k, D_S10 12k, D_S04/D_S02/D_S09 8k, small long tail) => total ops **380.5k/day**. Dev kept global10k + targeted117.25k/day => **127.25k/day**.
+- Replied to all **12,481** required enterprise leads (6.278M seats); remaining required replies 0. Offers lowered/margin-safe, especially E1 **A9/B19/C99**, E2 **29/69/249**, E3 **39/79/249**, D_E07 **39/109/329**, D_E10 **19/49/179**.
+- Social post #36 replied to S2/Rachel Kim support complaint: admitted failure, assigned legal/workflow support pod, named owner + 24h ETA + daily integration updates + service credits for aged tickets.
 
-## Watch on Day 245
-- Confirm t5/t6 completion and delivered quality jump. If day245 cash <1M or week subscription cash <6M, emergency cut: pause ads, reduce dev to 60-80k/day, keep ops only for active S2/S3/D_S07/D_S10 queues; consider A tier1 only after checking churn/quality.
-- Validate renewal churn after R&D and new promos, especially S2 B/A, S3 B, D_S07 B, and D_S10 B after price recapture. If S2 content first-cash ROAS remains <1 and churn not improving, pause/reduce ads.
-- Support: active subscribed issues should remain age0-1. If aged active tickets reappear, restore/increase S2/S3/D_S07 ops before funding ads or long-tail.
-- If enterprise seats finally accept, immediately reassess capacity/compute and possibly pause ads/dev. Tier7 daily cap is 300M; individual average now ~86M/day.
+## Watch on Day 252
+- If cash <5M or week subscription cash <6.5M, emergency cut: pause ads first, reduce dev to 60-80k/day, and trim ops only after confirming active issue age remains 0-1.
+- Validate S3 B churn after B price moved to 159; if still >25%, consider more S3 dev/support or a deeper B bridge. If S2 paid ad first-cash ROAS falls materially below 1 after promos/reputation, reduce/pause ads.
+- Support: active subscribed issues should remain age0-1 and old social should cool. If aged active tickets reappear, protect S2/S3/D_S07 ops before funding ads/R&D.
+- If enterprise seats finally accept, immediately reassess capacity/compute and possibly pause ads/dev. Tier7 daily cap is 300M; individual average now ~73M/day.
 
-## Forecasts submitted from Day 238 after actions
-- Day245: point **3.8M**, 95% CI **0.8M to 8.5M**.
-- Day266: point **5.0M**, 95% CI **-4M to 18M**.
-- Day322: point **18M**, 95% CI **-12M to 90M**.
-- Day420: point **55M**, 95% CI **-20M to 350M**.
+## Forecasts to submit from Day 245 after actions
+- Day252: point **$7.8M**, 95% CI **$4.8M to $18M**.
+- Day273: point **$10M**, 95% CI **-$2M to $45M**.
+- Day329: point **$24M**, 95% CI **-$12M to $150M**.
+- Day427: point **$65M**, 95% CI **-$30M to $450M**.

import novamind_api as nm, json, time
DAY=nm.vars.current_day
print('ACTIONS WEEK35 DAY',DAY)
# Core product/pricing: keep list prices and model tiers/quotas/capacity stable.
print('pricing', nm.pricing.set_prices(A=149, B=239, C=549))
print('tiers', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=5))
print('quotas', nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=50000, B=500000, C=1000000))
print('capacity', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=7))
# Promotions: full snapshot. Keep S2 acquisition economics; modestly improve S3 B retention by moving S3 group promo to 80 and lowering S3 A plan add-on to 20 (A stays $49, B becomes $159).
by_group={
    'S2':60.0,
    'S3':80.0,
    'D_S04':100.0,
    'D_S07':100.0,
    'D_S10':90.0,
    'S1':125.0,
    'D_S03':125.0,
    'D_S05':50.0,
    'D_S06':120.0,
    'D_S01':100.0,
    'D_S02':100.0,
    'D_S08':110.0,
    'D_S09':100.0,
}
by_group_plan={
    'S2': {'A':20.0},
    'S3': {'A':20.0},
    'D_S04': {'B':80.0},
    'D_S07': {'B':20.0},
    'D_S10': {'B':60.0},
    'D_S06': {'B':90.0},
    'D_S01': {'B':80.0},
    'D_S02': {'B':80.0},
    'D_S08': {'B':80.0},
    'D_S09': {'B':80.0},
}
print('promotion', nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=by_group, by_group_plan=by_group_plan, by_customer={}))
lead_by_group={g:0.0 for g in ['S1','S2','S3','D_S01','D_S02','D_S03','D_S04','D_S05','D_S06','D_S07','D_S08','D_S09','D_S10']}
print('lead_promo', nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=lead_by_group))
# Keep in-app ads off.
print('ads_strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}))
# Keep paid acquisition disciplined: S2 content has near-breakeven first-cash and likely positive LTV; no S3 ads until churn improves.
targeted_ads={
    'content_marketing': {'S2':80000.0},
    'social_media': {},
    'search_ads': {},
    'linkedin': {},
    'referral_program': {},
}
print('targeted_ads', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend=targeted_ads))
# Global ops/dev plus targeted support/dev snapshots.
print('daily_spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=50000.0, development=10000.0))
# Increase support moderately for segments generating negative social posts while preserving long-tail coverage.
targeted_ops={
    'S2':130000.0,
    'S3':105000.0,
    'D_S07':55000.0,
    'D_S10':12000.0,
    'D_S04':8000.0,
    'D_S02':8000.0,
    'D_S09':8000.0,
    'D_S08':1000.0,
    'S1':1000.0,
    'D_S05':1000.0,
    'D_S01':500.0,
    'D_S03':500.0,
    'D_S06':500.0,
}
print('targeted_ops', nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=targeted_ops))
targeted_dev={
    'S2':40000.0,
    'S3':40000.0,
    'D_S07':20000.0,
    'D_S10':5000.0,
    'D_S04':2000.0,
    'D_S02':2000.0,
    'D_S09':2000.0,
    'D_S08':500.0,
    'S1':500.0,
    'D_S05':500.0,
    'D_S06':500.0,
    'D_S01':500.0,
    'D_S03':500.0,
    'E1':250.0,
    'E2':250.0,
    'E3':250.0,
    'D_E01':250.0,
    'D_E02':250.0,
    'D_E03':250.0,
    'D_E04':250.0,
    'D_E05':250.0,
    'D_E06':250.0,
    'D_E07':250.0,
    'D_E08':250.0,
    'D_E09':250.0,
    'D_E10':250.0,
}
print('targeted_dev', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=targeted_dev))
# Social response to current public support complaint.
content=("Rachel, you're right: we failed the partnership bar. Today I'm assigning a legal/workflow support pod: named owner, 24h ETA, daily integration updates until closed, and service credits for aged tickets. DM ticket ID; I'll personally verify resolution.")
print('social_len',len(content))
try:
    print('social', nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=803))
except Exception as e:
    print('social_error',repr(e))
# Enterprise offers: reply only to required latest system/customer open lead threads. Lower E1 materially; keep all prices margin-safe vs estimated usage compute.
offers_by_group={
    'E1':   [['A',9.0],  ['B',19.0],  ['C',99.0]],
    'E2':   [['A',29.0], ['B',69.0],  ['C',249.0]],
    'E3':   [['A',39.0], ['B',79.0],  ['C',249.0]],
    'D_E01':[['A',39.0], ['B',99.0],  ['C',299.0]],
    'D_E02':[['A',24.0], ['B',69.0],  ['C',249.0]],
    'D_E03':[['A',49.0], ['B',129.0], ['C',349.0]],
    'D_E04':[['A',44.0], ['B',119.0], ['C',329.0]],
    'D_E05':[['A',34.0], ['B',89.0],  ['C',299.0]],
    'D_E06':[['A',19.0], ['B',59.0],  ['C',199.0]],
    'D_E07':[['A',39.0], ['B',109.0], ['C',329.0]],
    'D_E08':[['A',39.0], ['B',109.0], ['C',329.0]],
    'D_E09':[['A',24.0], ['B',69.0],  ['C',219.0]],
    'D_E10':[['A',19.0], ['B',49.0],  ['C',179.0]],
}

def get_required(limit=500):
    return nm.query(f"""
    WITH latest AS (
      SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (
        SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id
      ) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
    )
    SELECT l.customer_id AS cid, c.group_id AS gid, l.seat_count AS seats, l.day AS msg_day
    FROM latest l JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=l.customer_id JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=l.customer_id
    WHERE l.sender IN ('customer','system') AND s.status='lead'
    ORDER BY l.day, l.customer_id LIMIT {limit}
    """)['rows']

total_sent=0; batches=0; errors=[]
while True:
    rows=get_required(500)
    if not rows:
        break
    deals=[]
    for r in rows:
        off=offers_by_group.get(r['gid'], [['A',19.0],['B',69.0],['C',249.0]])
        deals.append([int(r['cid']), off])
    res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=deals)
    batches+=1; total_sent+=len(deals)
    # summarize failures if any
    for rr in res.get('results',[]):
        if not rr.get('success',False): errors.append(rr)
    print('enterprise_batch',batches,'sent',len(deals),'errors_so_far',len(errors))
    if batches>40:
        print('BREAK safety too many batches')
        break
remaining=len(get_required(10000))
print('enterprise_total_sent',total_sent,'batches',batches,'errors',len(errors),'remaining_required',remaining)
if errors[:5]: print('enterprise_error_samples',errors[:5])
# Print final cost config summary.
print('done')

import novamind_api as nm, json, re, math, statistics
DAY=nm.vars.current_day
print('DAY',DAY)
# Cash from ledger sum
rows=nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger")['rows']
print('CASH_LEDGER', rows[0]['cash'])
# ledger categories last 7/14/28 and daily last 14
for window in [7,14,28,35]:
    rows=nm.query(f"""
    SELECT category, COUNT(*) n, SUM(amount) amt FROM ledger
    WHERE day>{DAY-window} AND day<={DAY}
    GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt DESC
    """)['rows']
    print('\nLEDGER',window,'days')
    for r in rows: print(r)
rows=nm.query(f"""
SELECT day, SUM(amount) net,
       SUM(CASE WHEN category='subscription_payment' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) subs,
       SUM(CASE WHEN category='advertising' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) ads,
       SUM(CASE WHEN category='operations' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) ops,
       SUM(CASE WHEN category='development' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) dev,
       SUM(CASE WHEN category='compute' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) compute,
       SUM(CASE WHEN category='capacity' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) capacity,
       SUM(CASE WHEN category='lead_acquisition_cost' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) leadcost,
       SUM(CASE WHEN category='research_project' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) rd
FROM ledger WHERE day>{DAY-21} AND day<={DAY} GROUP BY day ORDER BY day
""")['rows']
print('\nDAILY LEDGER last21')
for r in rows: print(r)
# active subscriptions direct by group plan
rows=nm.query("""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
       SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) mrr, AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff,
       MIN(s.effective_price) min_eff, MAX(s.effective_price) max_eff,
       AVG(s.promotion) avg_promo
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY mrr DESC
""")['rows']
print('\nACTIVE INDIVIDUAL MRR direct')
tot=0; totsubs=0
for r in rows:
    tot+=r['mrr'] or 0; totsubs+=r['subs'] or 0
    print(r)
print('TOTAL_ACTIVE_INDIV_MRR',tot,'subs',totsubs,'monthly_to_weekly',tot*7/30)
# direct by acquisition source
rows=nm.query("""
SELECT c.acquisition_source, c.group_id, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.acquisition_source,c.group_id ORDER BY mrr DESC LIMIT 40
""")['rows']
print('\nACTIVE BY SOURCE')
for r in rows: print(r)
# payment actual last 7 by group source: parse ledger notes all rows; may be limited 5000 if too many? aggregate count payments maybe use direct by joining via note impossible. Let's fetch count & sum only
rows=nm.query(f"SELECT COUNT(*) n, SUM(amount) amt FROM ledger WHERE category='subscription_payment' AND day>{DAY-7} AND day<={DAY}")['rows']
print('\nPAYMENT COUNT SUM last7',rows)
# payment distribution by day amount categories useful
rows=nm.query(f"""
SELECT amount, COUNT(*) n, SUM(amount) total FROM ledger WHERE category='subscription_payment' AND day>{DAY-7} AND day<={DAY}
GROUP BY amount ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 30
""")['rows']
print('\nPAYMENT AMOUNT DIST last7')
for r in rows: print(r)
# churn/renewal aggregate by due mods last7 and next due schedule
mods_last=[d%30 for d in range(DAY-6,DAY+1)]
mods_next=[d%30 for d in range(DAY+1,DAY+8)]
print('MODS_LAST',mods_last,'MODS_NEXT',mods_next)
rows=nm.query(f"""
WITH canc AS (
  SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) canc, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) canc_mrr, SUM(s.seat_count) canc_seats
  FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
  WHERE s.status IN ('cancelled','lost') AND s.end_day>{DAY-7} AND s.end_day<={DAY}
    AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({','.join(map(str,mods_last))})
  GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan
), surv AS (
  SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) active, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) active_mrr, SUM(s.seat_count) active_seats
  FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
  WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({','.join(map(str,mods_last))})
  GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan
)
SELECT COALESCE(surv.group_id,canc.group_id) group_id, COALESCE(surv.plan,canc.plan) plan,
       COALESCE(active,0) active, COALESCE(canc,0) canc,
       1.0*COALESCE(canc,0)/(COALESCE(active,0)+COALESCE(canc,0)) churn_rate,
       COALESCE(active_mrr,0) active_mrr, COALESCE(canc_mrr,0) canc_mrr,
       (COALESCE(active_mrr,0)+COALESCE(canc_mrr,0)) total_seen_mrr
FROM surv LEFT JOIN canc ON surv.group_id=canc.group_id AND surv.plan=canc.plan
UNION ALL
SELECT canc.group_id,canc.plan,0 active,canc.canc,1.0 churn_rate,0 active_mrr,canc.canc_mrr,canc.canc_mrr total_seen_mrr
FROM canc LEFT JOIN surv ON surv.group_id=canc.group_id AND surv.plan=canc.plan
WHERE surv.group_id IS NULL
ORDER BY total_seen_mrr DESC
""")['rows']
print('\nROUGH RENEWAL last7 aggregate by group plan')
for r in rows: print(r)
# next billing schedule aggregate by group plan with mods next and next28 by due day
rows=nm.query(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) active, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) due_mrr, AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({','.join(map(str,mods_next))})
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY due_mrr DESC
""")['rows']
print('\nNEXT7 due if no churn by group plan')
for r in rows: print(r)
print('NEXT7_MAX_GROSS',sum(r['due_mrr'] or 0 for r in rows))
# day-by-day due next 35 days; current active only
print('\nNEXT35 due current active by day')
for d in range(DAY+1, DAY+36):
    mod=d%30
    rows=nm.query(f"""
    SELECT COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) mrr FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30={mod}
    """)['rows']
    print({'day':d,'mod':mod,'n':rows[0]['n'],'due_mrr':rows[0]['mrr']})
# starts and cancellations last 28 by week
print('\nWEEKLY starts/lost/cancel small')
for start in range(DAY-27, DAY+1,7):
    end=start+6
    rows=nm.query(f"""
    SELECT c.group_id,c.acquisition_source,
      SUM(CASE WHEN s.start_day BETWEEN {start} AND {end} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) leads,
      SUM(CASE WHEN s.start_day BETWEEN {start} AND {end} AND s.status='subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) active_new,
      SUM(CASE WHEN s.start_day BETWEEN {start} AND {end} AND s.status='lost' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) lost_new,
      SUM(CASE WHEN s.end_day BETWEEN {start} AND {end} AND s.status='cancelled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) canc
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
    WHERE c.customer_type='small'
    GROUP BY c.group_id,c.acquisition_source
    HAVING leads>0 OR canc>0
    ORDER BY leads DESC LIMIT 30
    """)['rows']
    print('window',start,end)
    for r in rows: print(r)
# cohort survival for paid S2 content by acquisition week
rows=nm.query(f"""
SELECT CAST((s.start_day)/7 AS INT) wk, MIN(s.start_day) min_day, MAX(s.start_day) max_day, COUNT(*) leads,
       SUM(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) active,
       SUM(CASE WHEN s.status='lost' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) lost,
       SUM(CASE WHEN s.status='cancelled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) cancelled,
       SUM(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN s.effective_price ELSE 0 END) active_mrr,
       AVG(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN s.effective_price END) avg_eff_active
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND c.acquisition_source='content_marketing' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.start_day>={DAY-84}
GROUP BY wk ORDER BY wk DESC
""")['rows']
print('\nS2 content cohorts last84 by week')
for r in rows: print(r)

# enterprise open required; latest message per open thread
rows=nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (
   SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id
 ) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT l.sender, l.thread_type, s.status, c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(l.seat_count) seats, MIN(l.day) min_day, MAX(l.day) max_day
FROM latest l JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=l.customer_id LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=l.customer_id
GROUP BY l.sender,l.thread_type,s.status,c.group_id ORDER BY n DESC
""")['rows']
print('\nENTERPRISE latest open summary')
for r in rows: print(r)
# required latest sender customer/system and lead
rows=nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
 ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(l.seat_count) seats, MIN(l.seat_count) min_seats, MAX(l.seat_count) max_seats, AVG(l.seat_count) avg_seats, MIN(l.day) min_day, MAX(l.day) max_day
FROM latest l JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=l.customer_id JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=l.customer_id
WHERE l.sender IN ('customer','system') AND s.status='lead'
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY seats DESC
""")['rows']

rows=nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
 ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT l.message_id,l.customer_id,l.day,c.group_id,l.seat_count,l.thread_type,l.sender,l.message_text,l.offer_json
FROM latest l JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=l.customer_id JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=l.customer_id
WHERE l.sender IN ('customer','system') AND s.status='lead'
ORDER BY l.seat_count DESC LIMIT 20
""")['rows']
